phase3_findings.description: coverage_backing=backed asserted totality over required dimensions but evaluate_coverage's all() ran over whatever entries were recorded, so a truncated coverage return derived backed vacuously
an unestablished consumer-extension read silently shrank the authoritative dimension list while the run still recorded --render full and derived backed

four documented-falsehood sites: the anchor-cap comment claimed a record-time rejection the code downgrades instead; step-3-6-audit.md still stated the two-field query-triggers contract and either/neither wording; the renderer's complete-by-construction mode enumeration omitted enumerate-dimensions; the budget doc's headroom sentence still said ~4.3% at zero headroom
query-coverage's unestablished line was byte-identical across a corrupt-state arm and two benign arms, so a corrupt state read as 'no coverage round yet'
the anchor-floor downgrade discarded the computed floor token, so a tool-side text refusal was indistinguishable from the auditor's own substantive unestablished judgment

roughly half the fail-closed arms had no test: duplicate-key refusal, six of seven _validate_coverage read-boundary arms, the clean-round-with-no-coverage path, the absent coverage_render default, the coverage-side stdin token arms, record-coverage preconditions, and the legal-but-instruction-shaped anchor

fix_decisions.claim_text: model the audit dimensions as first-class keyed data the checklist render is one projection of, instead of regex-scraping bold-lead bullets out of rendered prose
fix_decisions.evidence: the deterministic key derivation from the rendered template is the operand issue #708's own acceptance criteria pin (stable identifier per rendered dimension); re-basing it on a new template data model is a template-format change well outside this diff

parking_evidence.basis: the proposed depth change would alter the AC-pinned key derivation and the template format, both outside this PR's scope
parking_evidence.failing_input: a prose reformat of a checklist bullet silently changing a durably-recorded key

fix_decisions.claim_text: the state owner cannot verify --expected-keys against the renderer, so an orchestrator passing only the returned keys makes totality vacuous
fix_decisions.evidence: the tool/orchestrator seam cannot be closed inside the state owner (it holds no template). Mitigated, not dismissed: an unenumerated key is refused, every missing enumerated key is synthesized unestablished, and the supplied keyset is now PERSISTED as coverage_expected so the claim is auditable. The unclosable remainder is stated verbatim in cmd_record_coverage docstring and docs/cutovers/issue-708-audit-coverage-growth.md, which is the bounded-honesty-scope acceptance criterion of the issue rather than an undisclosed gap.

phase3_findings.description: the degraded-render rule keyed on any non-appended render-status, but `absent` is a COMPLETE enumeration (no consumer extension authored) — so the coverage offer could never fire in a default consumer repo
step-3-6-audit.md still asserted query-findings was the tool one multi-line query, and omitted query-coverage from the query enumeration
the three unestablished coverage causes collapsed to one indistinguishable pair on the mandatory summary line
coverage recorded on a REVISE round returned an unqualified success echo for work no derivation can ever consume
query-coverage printed reason= conditionally, so an absent reason was indistinguishable from a truncated line
--expected-keys is orchestrator-supplied and unverifiable by the state owner; totality is only as good as that operand
